data.description:
Goal:
The knots-managed agent skills (knots, knots-create, knots-e2e, knots-plan-orchestrator) document neither the lease-based agent-identity model nor the handoff-capsule workflow. Add that coverage so agents bind a lease, claim under it, and attach handoff capsules correctly — instead of copying the deprecated `--*-agentname/model/version` flags from old command history.

Context (kno 0.16.1):
- Agent identity for notes, handoff capsules, and state transitions is declared by the BOUND LEASE. `--note-agentname/model/version`, `--handoff-agentname/model/version`, and `--agent-name/model/version` are DEPRECATED — IGNORED (they print a warning and are dropped).
- Correct flow: `kno lease create` -> `kno claim <id> --lease <leaseId>` (or `kno new ... --lease <leaseId>`) -> then `kno update <id> -H "<capsule>"` / `--add-note` inherit identity from the lease.
- Without a lease the author records as `[unknown <date>]`. Observed 2026-06-07: knot ff27 in the quilt project got `[unknown]` authorship on its handoff capsule because no lease was bound.
- The handoff-capsule mechanism (`kno update -H` / `--add-handoff-capsule`) is not mentioned in any skill today; knots-create only covers `kno new` (title / -d / --acceptance / --verification-step).

Verification:
1. `grep -ri "lease\|handoff" .claude/skills/knots*` (or wherever the skill sources live) -> present after the change (currently returns nothing).
2. Follow knots-create as written end to end -> the produced knot has a handoff capsule whose author is NOT `[unknown]`.
3. If `kno skills` distributes the skills, the installed copies reflect the new content.

Constraints:
- Keep guidance consistent across the skills that touch claim/advance/handoff (at least knots-create, knots, knots-e2e).
- Do not reintroduce the deprecated identity flags as examples.
